metadata.name: Toxic 1 — combat damage gives poison counter inline (no trigger on stack)
metadata.description: p1 controls Pestilent Syphoner (1/1 Flying, Toxic 1). p2 has no flying blockers. p1 declares Pestilent Syphoner as an attacker targeting p2. p2 declares no blockers. Combat damage resolves: Pestilent Syphoner (power 1, unblocked) deals 1 combat damage to p2, reducing p2's life from 40 to 39. Simultaneously, CR 702.164c causes p2 to receive 1 poison counter as an additional result of that same damage event — NOT via a trigger on the stack. Final state: p2 at 39 life with 1 poison counter; stack is EMPTY. This validates the core Toxic mechanic and contrasts it with Poisonous (CR 702.70a, which IS a triggered ability that goes on the stack) and Infect (CR 702.90b, which REPLACES damage with poison counters). Toxic is additive AND inline — the damage and the poison counter are concurrent results of the same damage step.
metadata.generation_notes: Pestilent Syphoner oracle text: '{1}{B} — Creature — Phyrexian Insect — 1/1 — Flying, Toxic 1'. Card definition exists at crates/engine/src/cards/defs/pestilent_syphoner.rs. CR 702.164a: Toxic is a static ability. CR 702.164c: 'Combat damage dealt to a player by a creature with toxic causes that creature's controller to give the player a number of poison counters equal to that creature's total toxic value, in addition to the damage's other results.' This means the poison counter is an inline result of the damage event — NOT a triggered ability placed on the stack. Key contrast: Poisonous (CR 702.70a) IS a triggered ability ('Whenever this creature deals combat damage to a player, that player gets N poison counters') which goes on the stack and requires priority_round + stack_resolve. Toxic does NOT go on the stack — after combat damage step, the stack is immediately empty and p2 already has 1 poison counter. Additionally, unlike Infect (CR 702.90b), Toxic does NOT replace life loss — p2 loses 1 life AND gets 1 poison counter. So p2 ends at 39 life (not 40) with 1 poison counter. Turn 4 used to avoid summoning sickness (CR 302.6). p2 has no creatures (and specifically no flying creatures) — Pestilent Syphoner's Flying is noted but not directly tested here. After combat damage: p2 life = 39, p2 poison_counters = 1, stack is empty (critical assertion distinguishing Toxic from Poisonous). SBA 704.5c: p2 has 1 poison counter (below 10) — no game loss. SBA 704.5a: p2 at 39 life (above 0) — no player death.

script.step_note: p1 declares Pestilent Syphoner as an attacker targeting p2. CR 508.1f: Pestilent Syphoner becomes tapped. Toxic is a static ability (CR 702.164a) — it does NOT trigger at declare_attackers. Both players pass priority.

actions.description: Initial: Pestilent Syphoner (1/1, Flying, Toxic 1) untapped on p1 battlefield; p2 at 40 life, 0 poison counters; stack empty
actions.note: Turn 4 — Pestilent Syphoner has no summoning sickness (CR 302.6). Toxic 1 is a static ability (CR 702.164a) — it does not trigger on attacking. p2 has no creatures, so no blockers. Stack is empty.

CR 508.1: p1 declares Pestilent Syphoner as an attacking creature targeting p2. CR 508.1f: Pestilent Syphoner becomes tapped as part of attacking. Toxic is static (CR 702.164a) — nothing goes on the stack at this point.

actions.description: After declare_attackers: Pestilent Syphoner is tapped; stack empty — Toxic is static, no trigger
actions.note: CR 508.1f: Pestilent Syphoner tapped upon attacking. CR 702.164a: Toxic is a static ability — it does NOT produce a triggered ability at declare_attackers or at any other time. Stack remains empty.
CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority with empty stack — advance to declare_blockers step.

script.step_note: p2 has no creatures and declares no blockers. Pestilent Syphoner becomes an unblocked attacker (CR 509.1h). Toxic does not trigger — it is a static ability (CR 702.164a). Both players pass priority — advance to combat damage.

actions.note: CR 509.1: p2 declares no blockers (no creatures on p2's battlefield, and no Flying creatures specifically). CR 509.1h: Pestilent Syphoner has no blocking creatures — it becomes an unblocked attacker. Toxic does NOT trigger here — CR 702.164a: Toxic is a static ability, not a triggered ability.

CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority with empty stack — advance to combat damage step. Pestilent Syphoner (power 1, Flying, Toxic 1, unblocked) will deal 1 combat damage to p2. CR 702.164c: that damage also causes p2 to receive 1 poison counter inline — not via a trigger.

script.step_note: Combat damage step (CR 510.1): Pestilent Syphoner (power 1, unblocked) deals 1 combat damage to p2. CR 120.3: p2 loses 1 life (40 → 39). CR 702.164c: Toxic 1 simultaneously causes p2 to get 1 poison counter as an additional result of the damage — this is NOT a triggered ability and does NOT go on the stack. After damage resolves: p2 at 39 life, p2 has 1 poison counter, stack is EMPTY. This is the critical distinction from Poisonous (CR 702.70a), which IS a trigger that goes on the stack.

actions.description: After combat damage resolves: p2 life = 39 (lost 1); p2 poison_counters = 1 (from Toxic 1, inline — not a trigger); stack is EMPTY
actions.note: CR 510.1b: Pestilent Syphoner (power 1, unblocked) deals 1 combat damage to p2. CR 120.3: p2 loses 1 life (40 → 39). CR 702.164c: Toxic 1 gives p2 exactly 1 poison counter as part of the same damage event — inline, not via the stack. Stack is EMPTY — this is the key difference from Poisonous (CR 702.70a), which would put a triggered ability on the stack requiring a separate priority round and stack_resolve.

CR 704.3: SBAs checked after combat damage. CR 704.5c: p2 has 1 poison counter — below 10, so 'player with 10+ poison counters loses' SBA does not apply. CR 704.5a: p2 life is 39 (above 0) — no player death SBA. CR 704.5g: Pestilent Syphoner (1/1) dealt damage to a player, not a creature — no damage is marked on Pestilent Syphoner — no lethal damage SBA on it. No SBAs fire.
CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority after combat damage and SBA check. Stack is empty — advance to end of combat.

actions.description: Final: p2 at 39 life (lost 1 from normal combat damage — Toxic does NOT replace damage like Infect); p2 has 1 poison counter (from Toxic 1, inline with combat damage — stack was never used); stack empty; Pestilent Syphoner still on p1 battlefield (tapped)
actions.note: CR 702.164c: Toxic 1 gave p2 exactly 1 poison counter inline with the combat damage — no stack interaction. p2 life = 39 confirms Toxic is ADDITIVE (normal life loss still applies), unlike Infect (CR 702.90b) which replaces life loss. Stack is empty confirms Toxic is NOT a triggered ability, unlike Poisonous (CR 702.70a) which would have left a trigger on the stack requiring resolution. p2 has 1 poison counter — below 10, so no game loss (CR 704.5c). Pestilent Syphoner survives (dealt damage to a player, not a creature — no damage marked on it).
